user.title: Pick a YKK SCK lock
user.description: Choose the lock you want to add. Only locks currently broadcasting BLE adverts visible to Home Assistant are listed.

adapters.title: Pick Bluetooth adapters for each role
adapters.description:
**Long-range** (read-only): scanner used to listen for the lock's state broadcasts. Any scanner that hears the lock will do — pick one with a stable signal.

**Short-range** (read/write): scanner used to send lock / unlock commands. Must be physically close to the lock (≤ ~3 m) to bond reliably. Typically the ESPHome `bluetooth_proxy` near the door.

Leave either as **Auto** if you only have one adapter today, or want HA to pick the best available scanner.

register.title: Register with the lock
register.description:
About to register with **{name}** (`{address}`).

1. Walk up to the lock so the Home Assistant Bluetooth adapter (or a nearby `bluetooth_proxy`) has line-of-sight (under ~3 m).
2. **Put the lock into smartphone registration mode** (`スマートフォン登録モード`). Follow the manufacturer's instructions for your model, or trigger it from an already-paired smartphone. On the SCK3025: remove the bottom cover, hold the registration button, and lock/unlock the **bottom** thumb-turn twice (YKK doors have two — the upper one will not work) — the lock should announce `スマートフォン登録モード`. Plain `登録モード` is for physical keys and will not work here.
3. Choose a 6-digit PIN below, then submit immediately — the lock leaves registration mode after a short window.

The handshake captures this lock's unique advertising key; without it, ongoing state cannot be decoded.

data.name: Smartphone name shown on the lock

abort.no_devices_found:
No YKK SCK locks are currently advertising.

An SCK lock that has never been paired with a smartphone stays silent on BLE — it only starts broadcasting once it's in registration mode (or already bonded). Put the lock into **smartphone registration mode** (`スマートフォン登録モード`) and retry.

Also confirm Home Assistant's Bluetooth adapter (or a `bluetooth_proxy`) is close enough to the lock.

To debug: *Settings → Devices & Services → Bluetooth → Advertisement monitor* and search by the lock's MAC prefix (e.g. `C2:46…` on the SCK3025; prefix varies by model). If the lock doesn't appear there, it's not reachable yet — it won't appear here either.
abort.unsupported_platform: Home Assistant is running on a platform the underlying Bleak library does not support (Android). Use a Linux/macOS/Windows host or an ESPHome bluetooth_proxy.

error.registration_failed: Registration failed. Make sure the lock is in smartphone registration mode (`スマートフォン登録モード`) and try again.
error.timeout: The lock did not respond in time. Move closer and retry while the lock is in registration mode.
error.stale_bond:
Bluetooth error 133 — usually a stale pairing on the Home Assistant side (e.g. the lock was reset since you last paired). SSH into Home Assistant and clear it with:

```
bluetoothctl remove <lock MAC>
```

Then put the lock back into smartphone registration mode and retry. If you keep hitting this, please open an issue at https://github.com/rokusei/ha-ykk-door/issues.
error.unknown: Unexpected error — check the Home Assistant logs. If it persists, please open an issue at https://github.com/rokusei/ha-ykk-door/issues.

init.title: Bluetooth adapters
init.description: Pick which scanner handles each role. Scanners currently seeing the lock are listed by RSSI; sources you previously selected remain selectable even when not currently visible.
